name: Information Systems B.S.
description: BS in Information Systems — Technical core, IS core, breadth requirements, concentrations, and Heinz electives.

categories.name: Technical Core (Prerequisites)
categories.description: Math, CS, and foundational courses required before IS core.

categories.name: IS Core
categories.description: Required IS core courses including colloquia, database, software engineering, digital transformation, and HCI.

categories.name: Breadth: Professional Comm
categories.description: One course from Professional Communications breadth category.
categories.notes: One course required from this list.

categories.name: Breadth: Quantitative Analysis
categories.description: One course from Quantitative Analysis & Research Methods breadth category.
categories.notes: One course required from this list.

categories.name: Breadth: Innovation & Entrepreneurship
categories.description: One course after completing 67-272 Application Design & Development.
categories.notes: Must complete 67-272 first, then take 67-443 or 94-491.

categories.name: Concentration: AI Management
categories.description: AI fundamentals, operationalizing AI, ethics, and summative course.
categories.notes: One from each sub-category: AI Fundamentals, Operationalizing AI, Ethics of AI, and a Summative Course. Heinz courses (94/95-XXX) available junior/senior year only.

categories.name: Concentration: Data Analytics
categories.description: Data cleaning/visualization, analysis, ML focus, and summative course.
categories.notes: One from each sub-category. 36-315 cannot double-count for breadth if used here. 05-434/11-344 can only count toward one sub-category. Summative: 94-489 (Heinz, junior/senior only).

categories.name: Concentration: Info Security
categories.description: Foundation, technical security, and policy/management courses.
categories.notes: Foundation (95-452 or 95-752), at least one technical security course, at least one policy/management course. All are Heinz courses — junior/senior year only.

categories.name: Concentration: UX
categories.description: Fundamentals, grid design, toolkit, and summative course.
categories.notes: One from each sub-category: Fundamentals, Grid Design, Toolkit, plus summative course 05-315. 05-452 cannot double-count for IS Core HCI requirement.

categories.name: Concentration: Product Mgmt
categories.description: Required UX research course, strategic decision making, and marketing/finance.
categories.notes: 67-368 required, plus one strategic decision making course and one marketing/finance course. Heinz courses available junior/senior year only.

categories.name: Heinz Courses (Jr/Sr)
categories.description: Heinz College courses available to IS students in junior and senior years.
categories.notes: IS students can only take Heinz courses in their junior and senior years.

categories.name: IS Electives (Optional)
categories.description: Optional IS department courses not required for graduation.
categories.notes: These are optional courses — not required for graduation but available to IS students.
